channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Come trasformare l’AI in un successo per le vendite: tutti i segreti e le applicazioni-chiave
item.description: Il 65% delle aziende mondiali sta usando l’intelligenza artificiale generativa su base regolare, quasi il doppio di un anno fa. Le aspettative sono alte: il 75% afferma che questa tecnologia trasformerà profondamente ogni settore industriale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Rizz dating app cofounder explains how AI works as a wingman
item.description: Can artificial intelligence be an effective wingman? Roman Khaves, cofounder and CEO of Rizz, thinks so. The AI dating assistant app analyzes screenshots of conversations you’re having on other platforms—from Tinder to iMessage—and crafts reply suggestions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: A robot begins removal of melted fuel from the Fukushima nuclear plant. It could take a century
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A long robot entered a damaged reactor at Japan's Fukushima nuclear power plant on Tuesday, beginning a two-week, high-stakes mission to retrieve for the first time a tiny amount of melted fuel debris from the bottom.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apple embraces the AI craze with its newly unleashed iPhone 16 lineup
item.description: CUPERTINO, Calif. (AP) — Apple on Monday charged into the artificial intelligence craze with a new iPhone lineup that marks the company’s latest attempt to latch onto a technology trend and transform it into a cultural phenomenon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The iPhone 16 family brings A18 and A18 Pro chipsets, with a serious boost to processing power
item.description: Apple’s A18 and A18 Pro chipsets have landed with the new iPhone 16 lineup, and the company claims it’s managed some impressive performance improvements.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: 10 former top military officials call Trump 'a danger,' back Harris
item.description: Ten retired U.S. military generals and admirals are backing Vice President Harris and warning against picking former President Trump in the upcoming election, according to a new letter.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Google Faces Antitrust Lawsuit Over Ad Tech, Potential Breakup Looms
item.description: Google's advertising technology is under scrutiny by the Justice Department and a coalition of states, which claim the company has a monopoly over online ad tech. The case could result in Google being forced to break up its ad tech business.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Google faces a new antitrust trial after ruling declaring search engine a monopoly
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — One month after a judge declared Google's search engine an illegal monopoly, the tech giant faces another antitrust lawsuit that threatens to break up the company, this time over its advertising technology.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google’s ad empire under fire with US antitrust trial to begin
item.description: The trial, which will take place in Alexandria, Virginia, marks the first case brought by the Biden administration against Big Tech to have its day in court.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Passport Photo Pitfalls: The Unlikely Consequences of Looking "Hot" in Your Travel ID
item.description: A growing trend on TikTok showcases people going all out with glam looks just to take their passport photos, but experts warn that this approach can lead to frustrating and even missed flights.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Conservative groups urge lawmakers to bolster US technology protections against China
item.description: More than a dozen conservative groups Monday wrote to lawmakers urging them to take action to protect U.S. innovation from Beijing as the House sets up a series of votes to crack down on China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Will genAI kill the help desk and other IT jobs?
item.description: As AI adoption continues to soar, corporate executives are being forced to make hard decisions on what IT jobs can be automated by the fast-evolving technology and which ones can’t.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Balance between innovation and market adaptability key to Tencent Cloud’s AI success
item.description: Tencent Cloud’s expansion in Asia Pacific (APAC) reflects its strategic efforts to capitalize on the growing demand for Artificial Intelligence (AI) and cloud computing services.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Former director of Los Alamos National Laboratory dead after car crash in New Mexico
item.description: A former top official in U.S. nuclear weapons research at Lawrence Livermore and Los Alamos national laboratories has died after an automobile crash in New Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two astronauts are left behind in space as Boeing's troubled capsule returns to Earth empty
item.description: Boeing’s first astronaut mission ended Friday night with an empty capsule landing and two test pilots still in space, left behind until next year because NASA judged their return too risky.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Silicon Valley billionaire Thomas Siebel sued Enel SpA over trade secret theft allegations
item.description: C3, based in Redwood City and known for its data management and analysis software, accused Enel of misappropriation of trade secrets and breach of contract in an Italian civil suit. C3 says it is owed €2.1 billion ($2.3 billion).
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Google is blasted by UK watchdog for what it calls anti-competitive behavior through digital ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google was slammed Friday by U.K. regulators who say it's taking advantage of its dominance in digital advertising to thwart competition in Britain, ratcheting up pressure that the tech giant is facing on both sides of the Atlantic over its “ad tech” business practices.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweden joins countries seeking to end screen time for children under 2
item.description: Sweden says children under the age of 2 should not be exposed to any digital screens. The recommendations, issued by the Scandinavian country’s public health agency earlier this month as a new school year begins, are the latest in a worldwide effort to limit screen time for young children.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What is GitHub? More than Git version control in the cloud
item.description: GitHub is at heart a Git repository hosting service, i.e. a cloud-based source code management or version control system, but that’s just the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US widens indictment of Russians in 'WhisperGate' conspiracy to destroy Ukrainian and NATO systems
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department has widened its indictment of Russians in the so-called WhisperGate malware attacks aimed at destroying computer systems in Ukraine and 26 NATO allies including the United States.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top network and data center events 2024
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment (Elton John
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ticketmaster's pricing for Oasis tickets is under investigation in the UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s competition watchdog has launched an investigation into the way more than one million tickets were sold for next year's reunion concerts from iconic 1990s Britpop band Oasis.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yellen says ending Biden tax incentives would be 'historic mistake' for states like North Carolina
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen warned voters in the battleground state of North Carolina that they could lose jobs if Republicans weaken a signature Biden administration law that encourages investments in manufacturing and clean energy.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Doctor warns parents after kids burned by 'Jolly Rancher grapes'
item.description: Videos encourage viewers to melt hard candies on the stove before coating grapes with the hot liquid and freezing them.
item.dc:creator: Rolynn Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: SSA leaning into e-signatures as way to cut mountain of mail
item.description: SSA is implementing e-signatures capabilities for more than 30 forms and removing the signature requirement altogether for 13 forms. The post SSA leaning into e-signatures as way to cut mountain of mail first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Anthropic launches the Claude Enterprise plan
item.description: Anthropic has launched the Claude Enterprise subscription plan, enabling businesses to securely leverage their own corporate data in their interactions with its Claude large language model.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco, HPE, Dell announce support for Nvidia’s pretrained AI workflows
item.description: Cisco, HPE, Dell and others are looking to use Nvidia’s new AI microservice application-development blueprints to help enterprises streamline the deployment of generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Chief Data Officers Profiles in Excellence in Government 2024: Data Analytics & Artificial Intelligence Trends
item.description: What strategies and technology are driving data strategy in government? The post Federal Executive Forum Chief Data Officers Profiles in Excellence in Government 2024: Data Analytics & Artificial Intelligence Trends first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Ciena and Arelion achieve 1.6 Tb/s optical transmission milestone
item.description: In a landmark achievement for network bandwidth transport, Arelion and Ciena have successfully completed the world’s first 1.6 terabits-per-second (Tb/s) wavelengths data transmission in a live network field trial.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data resilience and zero trust: Continuity of operations no matter what
item.description: How is DISA navigating cybersecurity and zero trust? The post Data resilience and zero trust: Continuity of operations no matter what first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: US DOJ intensifies antitrust investigation into Nvidia with new subpoenas
item.description: The US Department of Justice (DOJ) has escalated its antitrust investigation into Nvidia by issuing subpoenas to the company and others in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Solar-powered blimp could bring high-speed internet to your area
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how the Sceye HAPS solar-powered blimp-like aircraft aims to provide high-speed internet to remote areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indonesia arrests a fugitive former Filipino town mayor wanted for illegal online gaming scams
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A dismissed town mayor who fled the Philippines after being accused of helping establish an illegal online gaming and scam center catering mostly to clients in China has been arrested near Indonesia’s capital, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ and EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Another New Jersey offshore wind project runs into turbulence as Leading Light seeks pause
item.description: Another offshore wind project in New Jersey is encountering turbulence. Leading Light Wind is asking the New Jersey Board of Public Utilities to give it a pause through late December on its plan to build an offshore wind farm off the coast of Long Beach Island.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk's Starlink backtracks and says it will comply with judge's order to block X in Brazil
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Elon Musk’s satellite-based internet service provider Starlink backtracked Tuesday and said it will comply with a Brazilian Supreme Court justice's order to block the billionaire’s social media platform, X.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Wireshark 4.4 boosts network protocol visibility
item.description: The open source Wireshark network protocol analyzer is among the most widely used technologies by networking professionals. Version 4.4, which became generally available on Aug. 28, has a number of new features aimed at making it even easier to use and better understand what’s running on a network.
item.dc:creator: Network World
